21.correct: Evaporation removes water from the surface into the air; it is NOT a way by which underground water reaches the surface.

wrong.A: Springs are a natural way groundwater emerges at the surface.
wrong.B: Wells bring groundwater up to the surface.
wrong.C: Tube wells pump groundwater to the surface.

21.fact: Springs, wells and tube wells all tap the water table, whereas evaporation is part of the loss side of the water cycle.

22.correct: Mohiniyattam, the graceful classical 'dance of the enchantress', originated in Kerala.

wrong.A: Tamil Nadu is home to Bharatanatyam.
wrong.D: Andhra Pradesh is home to Kuchipudi.

22.fact: Mohiniyattam is performed by women with gentle, swaying lasya movements.

23.correct: During Ramzan (Ramadan), Muslims fast from dawn (before sunrise, after the pre-dawn meal sehri) until sunset (when they break the fast at iftar).

wrong.B: The fast begins at dawn, slightly before sunrise.
wrong.C: Noon to midnight is not the fasting period.
wrong.D: 'Morning to evening' is imprecise; the fast runs from dawn to sunset.

23.fact: Ramzan is the ninth month of the Islamic calendar, ending with the festival of Eid-ul-Fitr.

24.correct: Tribal groups like the Santhals of Jharkhand are known for lively music using the flute and the dhol.

wrong.A: Karnataka is associated with Yakshagana, not Santhal music.
wrong.C: Gujarat is known for Garba and folk forms, not the Santhals.
wrong.D: Manipur is known for Manipuri dance.

24.fact: The Santhals are among India's largest tribal communities, spread over Jharkhand, West Bengal and Odisha.

25.correct: Using diameter = angular size (in radians) x distance: 0.5 degrees is about 0.0087 rad; 0.0087 x 3.8x10^8 m gives roughly 3,475 km, the Moon's diameter.

wrong.A: 3500 km is close but the precise value is about 3,475 km.

25.fact: The Moon's actual diameter is about 3,474 km, roughly a quarter of Earth's.

26.correct: The Kuiper Belt is a region of icy bodies beyond the orbit of Neptune, including dwarf planets like Pluto.

wrong.B: The asteroid belt (rocky bodies) lies between Mars and Jupiter.
wrong.C: It is a region of objects, not a comet's tail.
wrong.D: It is not the source region of the solar wind (that is the Sun).

26.fact: Many short-period comets originate from the Kuiper Belt.

27.correct: The Mars Orbiter Mission (Mangalyaan) made India the first country to reach Mars orbit on its very first attempt (2014).

wrong.A: The PSLV is a launch vehicle, not the Mars mission itself.

27.fact: Mangalyaan was also one of the most cost-effective interplanetary missions ever flown.

28.correct: South Korea and Japan were the first Asian countries to host the FIFA World Cup, co-hosting it in 2002.

wrong.B: Japan and China did not co-host a World Cup.
wrong.C: China and Vietnam have not hosted the World Cup.
wrong.D: Malaysia and Myanmar have not hosted it.

28.fact: The 2002 World Cup was the first to be co-hosted by two countries; Brazil won the title.

29.correct: The third BRICS Summit (2011) was held in Sanya, China.

wrong.A: Moscow did not host the third BRICS summit.
wrong.C: New Delhi hosted the fourth BRICS summit (2012).

29.fact: South Africa joined the grouping in 2010-11, turning BRIC into BRICS.

30.correct: Oxygen in the atmosphere is replenished mainly through photosynthesis by plants, algae and cyanobacteria.

wrong.B: Combustion consumes oxygen rather than producing it.
wrong.C: Industrial activities consume oxygen and emit pollutants.
wrong.D: Respiration also consumes oxygen and releases carbon dioxide.

30.fact: Photosynthesis uses carbon dioxide and water with sunlight to produce glucose and release oxygen.

31.correct: In 2025, Rohit Sharma and Virat Kohli announced their retirement from Test cricket.

wrong.A: Sachin Tendulkar and Sourav Ganguly retired years earlier.
wrong.C: Shubman Gill remains an active Test player.
wrong.D: Rahul Dravid and M.S. Dhoni had already retired from Tests earlier.

31.fact: Both continued to be available for limited-overs (ODI) cricket after their Test retirements.

32.correct: Madhya Pradesh and Odisha were the theme states of the 38th Surajkund International Crafts Mela 2025.

wrong.A: Gujarat and Rajasthan were not the 2025 theme states.
wrong.C: Kerala and Karnataka were not chosen for 2025.
wrong.D: Punjab and Haryana were not the theme states.

32.fact: The Surajkund Mela is held annually in Faridabad, Haryana, showcasing handicrafts and culture.

33.correct: The Himalayan region is most prone to earthquakes and landslides, because it lies along an active plate-collision zone.

wrong.A: The Indo-Gangetic plain is relatively stable, though some areas feel Himalayan quakes.
wrong.C: The peninsular plateau is geologically old and stable.
wrong.D: Coastal plains face cyclones/floods rather than frequent earthquakes.

33.fact: The ongoing collision of the Indian and Eurasian plates makes the Himalayas seismically very active.

34.correct: The Penna (Pennar) river flows through Gandikota, often called the 'Grand Canyon of India', in Andhra Pradesh.

wrong.A: The Krishna does not carve the Gandikota gorge.
wrong.D: The Godavari does not pass through Gandikota.

34.fact: The Gandikota gorge is carved by the Penna river through the Erramala hills.

35.correct: A charged particle entering a uniform magnetic field perpendicular to it moves in a circular path, because the magnetic force always acts perpendicular to its velocity.

wrong.A: It does not move in a straight line; a force acts on it.
wrong.C: A helical (spiral) path occurs when the velocity has a component along the field, not perpendicular.
wrong.D: The motion is well-defined (circular), not random.

35.fact: This circular motion is the basis of devices like the cyclotron.

36.correct: The Zabt revenue system was not applied in provinces like Gujarat and Bengal mainly because of the inability to survey and measure the land accurately there.

wrong.A: Poor soil quality was not the reason for skipping Zabt.
wrong.B: Continuous foreign invasions were not the cause.
wrong.D: It was not due to a lack of local administration as such, but the difficulty of land measurement.

36.fact: The Zabt system, developed under Akbar (Todar Mal), fixed revenue based on measured land and average yields.

37.correct: India's new National Sports Policy 2025 replaces the existing National Sports Policy of 2001.

wrong.A: There was a 1984 policy, but the one being replaced is the 2001 policy.
wrong.C: The National Health Policy is unrelated to sports.
wrong.D: The National Youth Policy is a separate document.

37.fact: The National Sports Policy aims to broaden participation and improve India's performance at international events.

38.correct: In the Union Budget 2025-26, Bihar was chosen for the establishment of a Makhana (fox-nut) Board, as the state is India's largest makhana producer.

wrong.A: Uttar Pradesh was not selected for the Makhana Board.

38.fact: Bihar accounts for the bulk of India's makhana (fox-nut/lotus seed) production.

39.correct: The 'Bharat Energy Stack', aimed at creating a digital backbone for the power sector, was launched by the Ministry of Power.

wrong.B: The Ministry of Defence is unrelated to the power-sector digital stack.
wrong.C: The Ministry of Electronics & IT is not the launching ministry here.
wrong.D: The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy did not launch this initiative.

39.fact: A digital 'stack' provides standardised data and interoperability, like the India Stack in finance.

40.correct: In tennis, when a player softens the shot to make the ball drop just over the net into the opponent's court, it is called a drop shot.

wrong.B: A smash is a powerful overhead shot, not a soft one.
wrong.C: A slice is a shot with backspin, not specifically a soft drop over the net.
wrong.D: 'Two-hand' refers to a grip (e.g. two-handed backhand), not this shot.

40.fact: A well-disguised drop shot exploits an opponent positioned far behind the baseline.
